title: Death-Hunter Cyborg
content:
{{Top|leg}}
[[File:DeathHunter.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A Death-Hunter Cyborg equipped with a [[turbocannon]].]]
The '''Death-Hunter Cyborg''' was the brain-child of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Jesco Comark]], who used his personal position in [[Renik]] to siphon funds for his pet operation.

==History==
Comark did not believe that the Galactic Empire could rely on [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] for its 'dirty work' and therefore worked to create perfect killing machines. The project, [[Operation Death-Hunter]], involved the kidnapping of strong, healthy [[Human/Legends|Humans]] of ages 15 to 25 from the nearby world of [[Bescane/Legends|Bescane]]. The subjects would then be '[[Cyborg/Legends|cyborged]]', which resulted in all physical abilities augmented by technological additions. This meant that their eyesight was near perfect with lowlight and macrozoom capabilities as well as specialized receptors to improve hearing. Lifeform sensors were added to further aid the Death-Hunter on its mission. Communication additions could also be added with a choice from wideband receiver and a tightband antenna. These assassins could also make use of [[Tech 4 PDV|Plexus courier droid]] to serve as transports, Comark did this through the use of the [[Jaemus shipyards|shipyards]] at [[Jaemus/Legends|Jaemus]]. The cyborgs were equipped with prototype [[turbocannon]]s and saw service alongside Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]].<ref name="DH">{{JournalCite|9|Death-Hunter}}</ref>

The project came to an end due to the destruction of [[Underwater Zeta Zero Nine base|Comark's base]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Zeta Zero Nine]] located in [[the Wastes]] at the hands of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. This was done with the aid of Comark's own daughter, [[Amber Comark]], who exposed the project.<ref name="DH" />
